tluaNumberKeyDuplicates.tlua(2,25): error TS1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
tluaNumberKeyDuplicates.tlua(9,28): error TS1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
tluaNumberKeyDuplicates.tlua(12,27): error TS1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.


==== tluaNumberKeyDuplicates.tlua (3 errors) ====
    // [1] written twice names the same number key: duplicate.
    local dup1 = { [1] = 1, [1] = 2 };
                            ~~~
!!! error TS1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
    
    // Number key 1 and string key "1" are distinct keys: no duplicate.
    local ok1 = { [1] = 1, ["1"] = 2 };
    local ok2 = { [1] = 1, ["1"] = 2 };
    
    // Spelling canonicalization: 0x10 and 16 name the same key.
    local dup2 = { [0x10] = 1, [16] = 2 };
                               ~~~~
!!! error TS1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
    
    // 1.0 and 1 name the same key.
    local dup3 = { [1.0] = 1, [1] = 2 };
                              ~~~
!!! error TS1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
    
    local es = {dup1, ok1, ok2, dup2, dup3};
